subject: Human Resources
title: Recognition and Appreciation
description: Learn Recognition and Appreciation in one minute.

hook.text: An employee works nights to finish a major project. No one says thank you. Next time, they won't bother.

buildup.text: Recognition validates effort—telling employees their work is seen, valued, and matters to the organization.

discovery.text: Employees who receive regular recognition are 5x more likely to feel connected and 4x more likely to be engaged.

twist.text: Recognition doesn't need to be expensive. A specific, timely 'thank you' from a manager is more powerful than a bonus.

climax.text: Public recognition amplifies the effect—it signals to everyone what behaviors the organization values.

punchline.text: What gets recognized gets repeated. Silence kills motivation.

quiz.question: What form of recognition is often most powerful?
options: A specific, timely thank you from a manager
